title: Deep learning training dynamics analysis for single-cell data
methods: deep neural network training dynamics analysis
data_sources: pancreatic beta-cell scRNA-seq from diabetic mouse models (Fltp)

data_scope.accession_numbers: not publicly specified in accessible article metadata (Hybrid Access)

core_conclusion: Annotatability uses deep neural network training dynamics — per-cell confidence and variability across training epochs — to classify pre-annotated single-cell and spatial omics cells as correctly annotated, erroneously annotated, or ambiguous. Applied to eight datasets, it identified annotation errors, revealed intermediate cell states and continuous trajectories from binary labels, and discovered disease-associated subpopulations, without requiring additional experimental data.
why_it_matters: Annotations in public single-cell atlases are noisy and oversimplify biological continuity; Annotatability provides a principled, modality-agnostic audit of those labels. It transfers a concept from NLP/CV (dataset cartography) into biology for the first time. The ambiguity signal recovers continuous biological processes (e.g., EMT) from discrete labels. Validated across 8 diverse datasets spanning immune, pancreatic, and epithelial biology.
why_nature: Cross-domain conceptual transfer from NLP/CV training-dynamics research to single-cell biology is a high-level framework contribution. The method is universally applicable to any pre-annotated omics dataset. It uses the DNN interpretively, not as a black-box predictor, aligning with the journal's emphasis on interpretable computation. Published as a Research Briefing — a format designed for compact, high-signal methodological contributions.
added_value_to_my_research: Training-dynamics auditing of geospatial crowdsourced labels (OSM POIs, land-use) to produce principled label-quality maps for GeoAI applications
Ambiguity signal as a detector of continuous spatial gradients disguised by discrete administrative boundaries — refining MGWR bandwidth near regime-transition zones
Confidence/variability curriculum for fine-tuning geospatial foundation models — exclude mislabeled training locations, up-weight high-quality ones
Multi-source training-dynamics comparison across data modalities (mobile phone, remote sensing, social media) to diagnose spatial data-fusion conflicts
Spatial distribution of hard-to-classify predictions as a proxy for health-outcome complexity and healthcare access deficits in 2SFCA/R2SFCA service-area analysis

figures.caption: Fig. 1: Interpreting single-cell data using Annotatability. (a) Mean inferred annotation probability by training epoch for CD14+ monocytes in the PBMC scRNA-seq dataset — correctly annotated cells (blue) learned rapidly, ambiguous intermediate monocytes (orange) learned slowly and conflated with FCGR3A+ monocytes, erroneous cells (red) not overfit until late epochs. (b) 2D confidence-variability map for pancreatic beta-cells from diabetic mouse models — hard-to-learn subpopulation (bottom left) identified as insulin-treated cells with healthy-like beta-cell marker profiles.
